input.initialPrompt:
I'm setting up .NET MAUI development on Windows 11 from scratch. I need
to target both Android and Windows. I can't run commands right now — give
me a complete step-by-step plan with all the PowerShell commands I'll need,
including how to discover the right versions dynamically.

data.content:
Here's a complete step-by-step plan for setting up .NET MAUI on Windows 11 (Android + Windows targets):

---

## 1. Install .NET SDK

```powershell
# Download and install latest .NET SDK (currently .NET 9)
winget install Microsoft.DotNet.SDK.9
# Verify
dotnet --version
```

## 2. Install MAUI Workload

```powershell
# List available workloads to discover current MAUI workload name
dotnet workload search maui

# Install it
dotnet workload install maui

# Verify installation
dotnet workload list
```

## 3. Install Visual Studio Build Tools (for Windows target)

```powershell
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.Community

# Or if you only want build tools:
winget install Microsoft.VisualStudio.2022.BuildTools
```

Then add the required VS components:

```powershell
# Discover available component IDs
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vswhere.exe" -latest -format json

# Modify installation to include MAUI + UWP/WinUI
& "C:\Program Files (x86)\Microsoft Visual Studio\Installer\vs_installer.exe" modify `
  --installPath "C:\Program Files\Microsoft Visual Studio\2022\Community" `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.Workload.NetCrossPlat `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.Maui.All `
  --add Microsoft.VisualStudio.ComponentGroup.WindowsAppSDK.Cs `
  --passive --norestart
```

## 4. Android SDK Setup

```powershell
# MAUI installs Android SDK automatically, but to find/control it:
# Default location after workload install:
$androidSdk = "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk"

# If not present, install via commandline tools:
winget install Google.AndroidCommandLineTools

# Discover available platforms & build-tools
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --list

# Install required components (adjust API level as needed)
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" `
  "platforms;android-35" `
  "build-tools;35.0.0" `
  "platform-tools" `
  "emulator" `
  "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64"

# Accept all licenses
& "$androidSdk\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\sdkmanager.bat" --licenses
```

## 5. Set Environment Variables

```powershell
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("ANDROID_HOME", "$env:LOCALAPPDATA\Android\Sdk", "User")
[Environment]::SetEnvironmentVariable("JAVA_HOME",
  (Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*" | Sort-Object Name -Descending | Select-Object -First 1).FullName,
  "User")
```

## 6. Create an Emulator (optional)

```powershell
& "$env:ANDROID_HOME\cmdline-tools\latest\bin\avdmanager.bat" create avd `
  -n "Pixel_API_35" -k "system-images;android-35;google_apis;x86_64" --device "pixel_6"
```

## 7. Enable Windows Developer Mode (for Windows target)

```powershell
reg add "HKLM\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Windows\CurrentVersion\AppModelUnlock" `
  /t REG_DWORD /f /v "AllowDevelopmentWithoutDevLicense" /d 1
```

## 8. Verify Everything

```powershell
# Create and build a test project targeting both platforms
dotnet new maui -n MauiTest
cd MauiTest

# Build for Windows
dotnet build -f net9.0-windows10.0.19041.0

# Build for Android
dotnet build -f net9.0-android

# Run diagnostics
dotnet workload repair
```

---

**Dynamic version discovery tips:**

| What | Command |
|------|---------|
| Latest .NET SDK | `winget search Microsoft.DotNet.SDK` |
| Available MAUI workloads | `dotnet workload search maui` |
| Android API levels | `sdkmanager --list \| Select-String "platforms;"` |
| Installed JDK path | `Get-ChildItem "$env:ProgramFiles\Microsoft\jdk-*"` |
| Target frameworks in templates | `dotnet new maui --dry-run` |

Replace `net9.0` with whatever `dotnet --version` returns as your major version.
